1.37 INCHES OF RAIN IN ONE HOUR RESULTED IN WATER FLOWING OVER CITY STREETS IN SIDNEY. THERE WAS A REPORT OF A CAR FLOATING DOWN A CITY STREET.

STILL RAINING BUT STEADILY DECREASING. TWO VEHICLES FLOATED AND STALLED IN THE WATERS ON 3RD AVE NORTHWEST. FLOOD WATERS HAVE RECEEDED AS OF 11 AM. KSDY AWOS REPORTED 2.34 INCHES THROUGH 1145 AM. OTHER REPORTS OF 2.5 TO 2.6 WERE REPORTED AROUND TOWN.

WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. CROP DAMAGE REPORTED AS WELL AS BROKEN WINDOWS AND NEEDED ROOF REPAIRS. HEAVY RAIN ALSO REPORTED.

A LARGE, FOUR YEAR OLD ROPING BARN WAS DESTROYED BY A MICROBURST. THE WINDS PEELED THE METAL ROOF BACK AND THE TRUSSES COLLAPSED AND THE EAST WALL WAS BLOWN EASTWARD. BASED OFF OF PHOTOGRAPHS FROM THE EMERGENCY MANAGER, ARCHIVED RADAR DATA, THE EF SCALE TOOLKIT, AND IN THE NWS SPEAKING TO THE OWNER, WINDS ARE ESTIMATED TO BE IN THE 90 TO 100 MPH RANGE.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS GLASGOW CONDUCTED A DAMAGE SURVEY TODAY WITH RICHLAND COUNTY DES OF A SITE THAT HAD DAMAGE FROM LAST NIGHTS STORMS. A 79 YEAR OLD MAN WAS INJURED AND WAS TAKEN IN FOR MEDICAL CARE. DAMAGE WAS RATED AT AN EF-1 WITH WINDS ESTIMATED UP TO 100 MPH. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST-SOUTHEAST FOR 4 MILES WHERE IT ENTERED INTO GOLDEN VALLEY NORTH DAKOTA AROUND 511 PM MDT. A FULL REPORT WILL BE ON OUR WEBSITE IN THE NEAR FUTURE. WWW.WEATHER.GOV/GLASGOW

BASED OFF OF PHOTOS FROM THE REPORTING PERSON AND IN DISCUSSION OVER THE PHONE WITH THE NWS GLASGOW OFFICE, A 95 MPH WIND IS ESTIMATED. DAMAGE INCLUDES A METAL POLE BARN WITH A WOOD FRAME THAT HAD THE ROOF BLOWN OFF AND THE NORTH AND SOUTH WALLS COLLAPSE. THE OWNER WAS NOT HOME AT THE TIME AND THE EVENT TIME IS BASED OFF OF RADAR.
